notes: With-skill achieves 100% pass rate across all 5 evals (20/20 assertions). Without-skill passes only 4/20 assertions (20%).
The three mandatory challenges (Strategic Override, Data Gap, What Would We Regret) are the strongest differentiators — they NEVER appear without the skill and ALWAYS appear with it.
Negative evals (sprint-planning, metrics-review) show perfect boundary enforcement with skill (redirects correctly) vs 0% without skill (answers directly).

Without-skill baseline correctly identifies RICE table structure (eval 1) and Value vs Effort quadrants (eval 3), showing Claude has general PM knowledge — but lacks the structured challenge framework.
The 'frequency of request assessed' assertion (eval 2) passes in both configurations — Claude naturally flags single-customer churn threats. This assertion may not strongly differentiate skill value.
Scoring assumptions visibility is a consistent differentiator: with-skill always shows detailed rationale; without-skill provides raw numbers only.
No timing/token data available (inline execution). Future runs should use subagent execution for resource measurement.
